steps.title: Scale the object count
steps.content: The total mass of $r$ identical objects is $t$. The new group has $146r$ objects, which is $146$ times as many objects.

steps.title: Scale the mass the same way
steps.content: For identical objects, total mass is proportional to the number of objects. If the object count is multiplied by $146$, the total mass is also multiplied by $146$: $$146\cdot t=146t$$

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content:
• Choices A and B subtract or add $146$, but mass must scale by multiplication.
  • Choice C, $\frac{t}{146}$, would make the mass smaller even though there are more objects.
  • Choice D, $146t$, multiplies the original mass by $146$. Correct.
That matches choice D.
